title: fix(e2e): harness setup nav uses domcontentloaded, not networkidle
body:
## Summary
The harness-api-key Gherkin scenarios were timing out on `page.waitForLoadState('networkidle')` after the backlog-provider work landed. App.tsx now mounts `useBacklogProvider` on every page; `useBacklogEvents` opens an SSE connection on epic-list/detail. Playwright's `networkidle` never settles within 30s because the SSE keeps the network 'active' indefinitely.

`domcontentloaded` is the right semantic here — the harness setup page is fully interactive once its DOM lands, and the assertion steps that follow each have their own per-element waits.

Closes the harness-api-key failures blocking the sandbox → main release PR #1721.

## Test plan
- [x] Lint clean
- [ ] CI green (Gherkin job)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

botReviews.body:
## Code Review: fix(e2e): harness setup nav uses domcontentloaded, not networkidle

**Files changed:** 1 | **+6 −1** | **Subagents:** Security, Consistency, Bug-Hunter

### Findings: 4

| # | Severity | File | Line | Rule | Summary |
|---|----------|------|------|------|---------|
| 1 | MAJOR | `ui/e2e/step-definitions/skill-usage.steps.ts` | 278 | consistency | `networkidle` used after page interaction in same app |
| 2 | MAJOR | `ui/e2e/step-definitions/chat.steps.ts` | 249 | consistency | `networkidle` in chat history page load assertion |
| 3 | MAJOR | `ui/e2e/step-definitions/chat.steps.ts` | 438 | consistency | `networkidle` in conversation list assertion |
| 4 | MINOR | `ui/e2e/step-definitions/backlog-import-milestone.steps.ts` | 234 | consistency | `networkidle` with `.catch(() => {})` masks SSE hang |

---

### #1 — MAJOR · `skill-usage.steps.ts:278`

Uses `waitForLoadState('networkidle')` on the same SPA that mounts `useBacklogProvider` / `useBacklogEvents` (SSE). Per this PR's rationale, SSE keeps the network perpetually active, so `networkidle` will hang until timeout. The same class of fix should apply.

**Suggested fix:** Replace `await this.page.waitForLoadState('networkidle')` with `await this.page.waitForLoadState('domcontentloaded')` or a targeted element visibility assertion, consistent with the harness-api-key fix.

### #2 — MAJOR · `chat.steps.ts:249`

Uses `networkidle` to wait for the chat history page to finish loading. The same SSE connection issue applies — `useBacklogEvents` keeps the network active. Should use `domcontentloaded` plus explicit element visibility checks (which the step already does immediately after).

**Suggested fix:** Replace with `await this.page.waitForLoadState('domcontentloaded')` — the subsequent locator assertions already gate on the content being rendered.

### #3 — MAJOR · `chat.steps.ts:438`

Same issue as #2 in the same file. The SSE connection prevents `networkidle` from resolving in a timely manner on any app page.

**Suggested fix:** Replace with `domcontentloaded` or remove entirely — the subsequent locator checks are sufficient.

### #4 — MINOR · `backlog-import-milestone.steps.ts:234`

Uses `networkidle` with `.catch(() => {})` to swallow errors, which is effectively a workaround for the same SSE timeout issue this PR fixes properly. The pattern is inconsistent with the new approach and silently hides the underlying problem.

**Suggested fix:** Replace `await this.page.waitForLoadState('networkidle').catch(() => {})` with `await this.page.waitForLoadState('domcontentloaded')` to be explicit and consistent.

---

**Note:** There are an additional 50+ `networkidle` calls in `*.spec.ts` files (git-export, reflections, skills, default-skills, evidence) that may also be affected by the SSE issue. Consider a follow-up PR to sweep those systematically.

---

Reviewed SHA: 3b933103812a050ae27e030688562c56fb42c357

## Code Review: fix(e2e): harness setup nav uses domcontentloaded, not networkidle

**Files reviewed:** 1 | **+7 / -1** | **SHA:** `5f25c92`

---

### Summary

Removes a `page.waitForLoadState('networkidle')` call that could never resolve because `useBacklogProvider` opens a persistent SSE connection on every page. Replaces it with a thorough comment explaining why no explicit wait is needed (`page.goto` already waits for `load`, and downstream assertion steps have their own element-level waits).

---

### Findings

| # | Severity | File | Line | Rule | Summary |
|---|----------|------|------|------|---------|
| 1 | NIT | `harness-api-key.steps.ts` | 29–35 | style | Comment block is 6 lines for a removed one-liner |

**Finding 1 — NIT (style):** The comment at lines 29–35 is thorough and well-written, but at 6 lines it's heavier than the code it replaces. Consider collapsing to 2–3 lines capturing just the key facts (SSE prevents `networkidle`; `goto` already waits for `load`; downstream steps self-wait). This is purely cosmetic — the existing comment is not wrong.

---

### Verdict

Clean, correct fix. The root cause is well-identified (SSE from `useBacklogEvents` keeps the network active), the solution is minimal (remove the impossible wait), and all downstream Gherkin steps use Playwright auto-waiting assertions (`toBeVisible`, `toContainText` with explicit `timeout`), so no flakiness is introduced.

**LGTM — ship it.**

Reviewed SHA: 5f25c9229ec78f4ab5508e85207cb12b5eb881ed
